symbols.name: \(K\)
symbols.def: Prespecified maximum number of latent confounder classes.

symbols.name: \(\mathcal U_K\)
symbols.def: Latent state space with \(K\) labelled classes.

symbols.name: \(\mathcal R\)
symbols.def: Joint mediator and outcome response-type alphabet constructed in \(def:response-alphabet\).

symbols.name: \(R\)
symbols.def: Joint mediator and outcome potential-response type constructed in \(def:response-alphabet\).

symbols.name: \(y_{am}(r)\)
symbols.def: Outcome value encoded by \(r\) under \(A=a\) and \(M=m\).

symbols.name: \(g\)
symbols.def: Generic function on \(\mathcal U_K\) used by the finite completeness operator.

symbols.name: \(\eta\)
symbols.def: Fixed analyst-specified lower bound on the squared maximal minors of the proxy measurement operator.

symbols.name: \(\kappa\)
symbols.def: Fixed analyst-specified lower bound on every latent-class probability.

symbols.name: \(\Delta_K(\lambda,q^W)\)
symbols.def: Sum of squared maximal \(\min\{\lvert\mathcal W\rvert,K-1\}\)-minors constructed in \(def:proxy-rank-separation\).

symbols.name: \(\gamma_r\)
symbols.def: Mass assigned to response type \(r\) in the no-proxy model.

symbols.name: \(\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)\)
symbols.space: linear maps from \(\mathbb R^K\) to \(\mathbb R^{|\mathcal W|}\)

symbols.name: \(\mathcal F_K(P)\)
symbols.space: subsets of a finite-dimensional compact set

symbols.name: \(\mathcal F_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\)
symbols.space: subsets of a finite-dimensional compact set

symbols.name: \(\mathcal E_{K,\mathrm{fail}}^L(P),\mathcal E_{K,\mathrm{fail}}^U(P)\)
symbols.def: Incomplete-proxy feasible triples attaining a no-proxy endpoint, constructed in \(def:endpoint-faces\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal P_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^+\)
symbols.def: Strictly positive binary observed laws with both treatment arms and a compatible three-active-class proxy-factorized representation whose binary outcome proxy has rank two and is therefore informative but incomplete for the active latent support.

novel.justification: A finite labelled latent-state model is meaningful in deliberately coarsened sensitivity analyses, where \(K\) denotes the retained number of substantively distinct unmeasured risk strata.

In a coarsened sensitivity analysis, retained risk strata are intended to be substantively present rather than padding labels. A fixed lower mass is appropriate when the analyst excludes arbitrarily rare latent strata from the model and retains compactness of the finite-table sensitivity region.

Separate pre-treatment measurements can record distinct manifestations of one latent clinical or social risk while the joint response type records the downstream mediator and outcome mechanism. Conditional independence is appropriate when their residual measurement errors arise in distinct instruments.

Three substantively present coarsened latent risk strata can generate two distinct prevalence profiles for a binary outcome-inducing proxy. Compatibility with the rank-two finite measurement model retains an informative proxy while a binary measurement cannot invert all three active strata. This is a falsifiable population restriction of the stated sensitivity model, not an assertion about either endpoint.

definitions.name: \(\mathcal F_K(P)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal F_K(P)=\{(\lambda,q^Z,q^W):\lambda_{ur}\ge0,\ \sum_{u\in\mathcal U_K,r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}=1,\ q^Z_{zu}\ge0,\ \sum_{z\in\mathcal Z}q^Z_{zu}=1,\ q^W_{wu}\ge0,\ \sum_{w\in\mathcal W}q^W_{wu}=1,\ p_{azwmy}=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_K,r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}q^Z_{zu}q^W_{wu}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r),y=y_{am}(r)\}\ \forall(a,z,w,m,y)\}\).

definitions.name: \(\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)\)
definitions.construction: \([\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)g](w)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_K}g(u)q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\) for \(g\in\mathbb R^K\) and \(w\in\mathcal W\).

definitions.name: \(\Delta_K(\lambda,q^W)\)
definitions.construction: \(\Delta_K(\lambda,q^W)=\sum_{\substack{I\subseteq\mathcal W,\ J\subseteq\mathcal U_K\,;\ \lvert I\rvert=\lvert J\rvert=\min\{\lvert\mathcal W\rvert,K-1\}}}\det\!\left([q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}]_{w\in I,u\in J}\right)^2\).

definitions.name: \(\mathscr H_3(P)\)
definitions.construction: For \(P\in\mathcal P_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^+\), \(\mathscr H_3(P)\) applies Tarski--Seidenberg quantifier elimination to the polynomial constraints defining \(\mathcal E_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^L(P)\) and \(\mathcal E_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^U(P)\), with the rank-two binary-proxy requirement written as nonvanishing of at least one \(2\times2\) minor of \(\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)\); Positivstellensatz infeasibility certificates provide a checkable route for a rational table.

statements.statement: For every observed law \(P\) generated under \(ass:treatment-randomization\), \(ass:treatment-positivity\), \(ass:mediator-consistency\), \(ass:outcome-consistency\), \(ass:k-latent-alphabet\), \(ass:active-latent-classes\), and \(ass:proxy-factorization\), the identified set for \(\psi=E[Y(1,M(0))-Y(0,M(0)]\) over the \(K\)-active-class SCM whose proxy representation belongs to \(\mathcal F_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\) is \(\Theta_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\), and the extrema \(L_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\) and \(U_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\) are attained whenever \(\mathcal F_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\ne\varnothing\).
statements.justification: The closed, quantitatively separated, active-class incomplete-proxy formulation supplies the well-posed latent-realizability substrate required to study endpoint exclusion after bridge inversion fails.
statements.gap: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the general sharp discrete-SCM polynomial-program representation; this lemma specializes it to a quantitatively informative but noninjective proxy measurement regime with all retained latent classes active rather than claiming a new generic representation theorem.
statements.consumer: It makes the binary-observed, three-active-class failed-completeness endpoint-exclusion question a finite observed-data semialgebraic problem.

statements.statement: Under \(lem:fixed-k-program\), \(\Theta_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\subseteq[L_0(P),U_0(P)]\), \(L_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)>L_0(P)\) if and only if \(\mathcal E_{K,\mathrm{fail}}^L(P)=\varnothing\), and \(U_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)<U_0(P)\) if and only if \(\mathcal E_{K,\mathrm{fail}}^U(P)=\varnothing\).
statements.justification: Endpoint-face exclusion is the exact property that an observable certificate must recognize in the noninjective-proxy model.
statements.gap: BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives sharp no-proxy mediation bounds; the lemma is a feasibility reformulation, not by itself a demonstrated new strict regime.
statements.consumer: It converts any certified incomplete-proxy endpoint-face infeasibility result into a strict contraction of the established no-proxy interval.

statements.statement: After discarding \(Z\) and \(W\), the program \(def:no-proxy-feasible-set\) yields the sharp randomized-treatment natural-direct-effect interval \([L_0(P),U_0(P)]\) in the simple-mediation model.
statements.justification: The reduction fixes the published no-proxy floor used by the endpoint-exclusion question.
statements.gap: Sjolander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 characterize this no-proxy floor; the proposition is a required reduction check.
statements.consumer: It ensures that an excluded endpoint is interpretable as additional information supplied by the proxies.

statements.statement: For \(P\in\mathcal P_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^+\), determine necessary-and-sufficient observable semialgebraic inequalities for \(\mathcal E_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^L(P)=\varnothing\) or \(\mathcal E_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^U(P)=\varnothing\), determine whether those inequalities reduce to signed-minor and rank conditions, and determine a rational \(P\in\mathcal P_{3,\mathrm{fail}}^+\) at which at least one event holds. Equivalently, the question asks whether a strictly positive binary two-proxy law with three active latent classes satisfying \(\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\ge\kappa\) for every \(u\in\mathcal U_3\), \(\operatorname{rank}\{\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)\}=2<3\), and \(\Delta_3(\lambda,q^W)\ge\eta\) can certify a strict tightening \(L_3^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)>L_0(P)\) or \(U_3^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)<U_0(P)\) of the BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 interval.
statements.justification: This is the nondegenerate observable content absent from a generic response-function encoding: the binary outcome proxy distinguishes two directions of latent variation while remaining unable to invert three active latent classes.
statements.gap: BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives the no-proxy interval, while proximal point-identification papers impose completeness. DorettiStanghellini2024 gives binary natural-effect intervals under its PC-CWD restriction. ZhangSu2024 gives valid proxy bounds from partial observability of a single proxy transition matrix for a causal-effect setting, but neither its information structure nor its target supplies the observed-mediator cross-world endpoint certificate. No cited result supplies an observable certificate or a strictly positive two-proxy, rank-two-for-three-active-class witness for exclusion of a natural-direct-effect endpoint over this factorization-constrained model.
statements.consumer: The result would let a binary mediation study decide from its observed proxy table whether two informative measurements can strictly shorten the direct-effect interval despite failure of full three-active-class outcome-proxy completeness.

sampling_model.units: Each unit carries latent \(U\) and response type \(R\).
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The core concerns population identified sets and an exact finite-table certificate; endpoint inference is outside scope.

tldr: The headline question is whether a strictly positive binary negative-control table can visibly exclude an endpoint of the sharp no-proxy natural-direct-effect interval when an informative binary outcome proxy has rank two and all three latent classes carry mass at least \(\kappa\). The supporting machinery is a three-active-class, proxy-factorized response-function program restricted to this quantitatively separated failure of injectivity: it translates endpoint shortening into emptiness of a polynomial endpoint face. The research task is to derive an exact observable semialgebraic certificate, determine whether it has a signed-minor/rank form, and construct a rational positive-cell witness of strict contraction.

project_justification.gap: Proximal mediation point-identification results require conditional completeness, while sharp mediation bounds retain cross-world response types but omit proxy-factorization restrictions. Existing direct- and indirect-effect bounds with unobserved or partly observed mediator-outcome confounding do not encode conditionally independent negative-control measurements or a three-active-class finite measurement model in which a binary proxy is informative yet noninjective. A generic discrete-SCM program does not establish that any nondegenerate observed proxy table actually excludes a mediation-bound endpoint.
project_justification.niche: The binary-observed, three-active-class model permits a direct observed-data question about whether two proxies rule out a no-proxy cross-world endpoint without bridge inversion, while a rank-two binary outcome proxy remains informative but cannot recover every active latent direction.
project_justification.fill: The project derives an exact binary endpoint-exclusion certificate and a rational strictly positive witness, using the quantitatively separated incomplete proxy-factorized response-function program with a fixed active-class mass lower bound only as the finite latent-realizability substrate.

related_work: DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 gives three proximal point-identification results and semiparametric inference for natural direct and indirect effects under conditional completeness-based mediation models. The present question concerns the residual factorization-constrained set when a binary outcome proxy is informative but cannot be injective for three active latent classes. BaiWuSunCui2026 similarly develops completeness-based proximal path-specific identification and inference in a recanting-witness setting; its categorical completeness discussion motivates the finite failure regime but it is not a claimed comparator for the binary certificate. BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 derives sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds; the proposed witness would be a strict tightening of that interval in a stated binary-observed, three-active-class incomplete-proxy model. DorettiStanghellini2024 derives binary natural-effect intervals under no unobserved exposure-mediator confounding, partially constant cross-world dependence, and semiparametric logistic models. Its PC-CWD restriction is neither implied by nor implies the present proxy-factorized three-active-class measurement restriction, so its intervals do not subsume the proposed observed-table endpoint question. ZhangSu2024 obtains valid causal-effect bounds without completeness from partial observability of the single-proxy transition matrix \(P(W\mid U)\). Its single-proxy information structure and single-world causal-effect target neither yield nor rule out the observed-mediator, two-proxy cross-world endpoint certificate. GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 Section 5.1 treats cross-world natural direct and indirect effects with a hidden mediator. It is informative because the target is also cross-world, but the proposal does not assert non-representability of its bounds; it asks an observed-mediator, two-proxy factorization question. HanLuoZhangGu2026 bounds controlled, natural direct, and natural indirect effects with partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders, using measured-confounder stratification and additional linear-model identification conditions; it does not impose negative-control proxy factorization or characterize exclusion of a no-proxy endpoint in the incomplete-proxy model. LuoGeng2016 gives bounds for direct and indirect effects with an unobserved mediator-endpoint confounder and a continuous endpoint; it contains neither proxy measurements nor the finite factorization constraint. DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the general response-function polynomial-optimization template, so the program itself is supporting machinery rather than the contribution. MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017, \emph{On Partial Identification of the Natural Indirect Effect}, distinguishes the cross-world restrictions relevant to natural effects. Sjolander2009 supplies the randomized-treatment no-proxy direct-effect floor.
honest_scope: The proposal is conditional on a \(K\)-active-class sensitivity model with each latent class carrying mass at least \(\kappa\), conditionally independent proxy measurements, and a fixed positive minor-separation constant \(\eta\). The headline certificate specializes to three active latent classes and binary observables, where \(\operatorname{rank}\{\mathsf C_W(\lambda,q^W)\}=2<3\) makes the outcome proxy informative but noninjective on the active support. It makes neither an unrestricted-cardinality nor an asymptotic-inference claim. The response-function representation and endpoint-face equivalence are supporting lemmas. It remains open whether the specified model admits a strictly positive rational strict-contraction witness and whether endpoint exclusion has a useful necessary-and-sufficient semialgebraic, rank-and-minor characterization.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Sharp no-proxy bounds are derived for natural mediation effects under unmeasured intermediate-variable confounding.
Completeness-based proximal models point-identify natural direct and indirect effects and support semiparametric inference.
Completeness-based proximal bridges identify path-specific effects with robust inference.
Binary natural-effect intervals are derived under no unobserved exposure-mediator confounding, partially constant cross-world dependence, and semiparametric logistic models.
Section 5.1 gives proxy partial-identification results for cross-world natural direct and indirect effects with a hidden mediator.
Bounds for direct and indirect effects are derived with partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders, with additional stratification and linear-model results.
Bounds for direct and indirect effects with an unobserved mediator-endpoint confounder and a continuous endpoint are derived.
Discrete SCM queries admit sharp polynomial-program representations over response functions.
Valid causal-effect bounds are obtained without completeness from partial observability of the single-proxy transition matrix \(P(W\mid U)\).
Sharp randomized-treatment bounds are available for natural direct effects with confounded intermediates.

literature_map:
DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proximal natural-effect point identification and inference under conditional-completeness mediation models.
BaiWuSunCui2026 gives completeness-based proximal path-specific identification and robust inference in a recanting-witness setting.
BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds.
DorettiStanghellini2024 derives binary natural-effect intervals under partially constant cross-world dependence and semiparametric logistic models; its restriction is distinct from the proxy-factorized measurement model.
ZhangSu2024 obtains valid causal-effect bounds without completeness using partial observability of the single-proxy transition matrix \(P(W\mid U)\); it does not encode the observed-mediator, two-proxy cross-world NDE feasibility problem.
GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 Section 5.1 treats cross-world NDE and NIE partial identification with a hidden mediator, so it is not characterized as single-world-only.
HanLuoZhangGu2026 bounds direct and indirect effects when some mediator-endpoint confounders are observed, but does not model negative-control proxy factorization.
LuoGeng2016 bounds direct and indirect effects with unobserved mediator-endpoint confounding and a continuous endpoint, without proxies.
DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the generic discrete response-function polynomial-program representation.
MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 is \emph{On Partial Identification of the Natural Indirect Effect}.
The unresolved contribution is a nondegenerate observed-binary, three-active-class certificate and rational positive-cell witness for strict factorization-based contraction when the binary outcome proxy has rank two but cannot be injective on the active support.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 core stopped at bridge-fiber geometry and did not supply latent-realizable endpoint exclusion, observable inequalities, or a positive-cell witness. The retained response program is explicitly supporting machinery; the kernel is the observed-binary, three-active-class failed-completeness certificate-and-witness question.
novelty_justification.published_axis: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 covers the generic response-function program, so no novelty is claimed for that representation. BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 supplies the no-proxy comparator. DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 and BaiWuSunCui2026 supply conditional-completeness proximal mediation identification. DorettiStanghellini2024 supplies binary natural-effect intervals under PC-CWD and semiparametric logistic models, not a proxy-factorized latent measurement restriction; neither model contains the other. ZhangSu2024 obtains valid causal-effect bounds from partial observability of one proxy transition matrix, not a cross-world natural direct effect with two proxy-factorized measurements. HanLuoZhangGu2026 and LuoGeng2016 bound mediation effects under measured or unmeasured confounding without negative-control proxy factorization. The unresolved claim is the factorization-specific observed binary endpoint-exclusion characterization and its nondegenerate rank-two-for-three-active-class witness.

literature_checklist.author: Dukes, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: Completeness-based proximal mediation identifies natural direct and indirect effects and gives multiply robust inference.

literature_checklist.author: Doretti and Stanghellini
literature_checklist.one_line: Binary natural-effect intervals follow from PC-CWD and semiparametric logistic models, a cross-world restriction distinct from the proxy-factorized three-class model.

literature_checklist.author: Breum et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Sharp no-proxy natural mediation bounds supply the endpoint comparator.

literature_checklist.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: Section 5.1 treats cross-world NDE and NIE bounds with a hidden mediator.

literature_checklist.author: Han, Luo, Zhang, and Gu
literature_checklist.one_line: Bounds with partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders use measured-confounder information but not negative-control proxy factorization.

literature_checklist.author: Luo and Geng
literature_checklist.venue: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
literature_checklist.one_line: Direct- and indirect-effect bounds with unobserved mediator-endpoint confounding do not use proxy measurements.

literature_checklist.author: Duarte et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Discrete SCM response-function programs provide the supporting finite optimization representation.

literature_checklist.author: Miles et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Natural-effect partial identification depends on cross-world restrictions.

literature_checklist.author: Bai et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Completeness-based proximal path-specific inference motivates the failure regime.

literature_checklist.author: Zhang and Su
literature_checklist.one_line: Proxy partial identification uses partial observability of one proxy transition matrix for causal-effect bounds without completeness, rather than a two-proxy cross-world NDE certificate.
